title: eupatorium purpureum var. ternifolium
intro: Eupatorium purpureum var. ternifolium, also known as Joe Pye weed, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that grows in eastern North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and reduce fever. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as supporting the immune system, relieving symptoms of colds and flu, and acting as an expectorant to ease respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium purpureum var. ternifolium can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to create topical creams and salves for skin conditions and wounds.
